prompt: If your writing style were a material (wood, brass, cotton, glass, concrete, etc.), which would it be and why?
response:
What a creative question! If my writing style were a material, I’d choose **glass**.

Glass is clear, letting light and meaning shine through with minimal distortion—just as I aim for clarity and transparency in my responses. It can be elegant or simple, adapting to different contexts, much like how I tailor my tone to your needs. Glass is also strong yet flexible, able to take many forms, just as I strive to be versatile and helpful. And, importantly, glass is something you can look through to see the world more clearly—just as I hope my answers help you see topics in a new light.

What about you—what material matches your communication style?
